architecture.summary: A recurrent neural network model that stores structured knowledge using vector symbolic architecture (VSA) combined with associative memory plasticity rules. Knowledge structures are represented as sets of binary relations between events and attributes, encoded into distributed neuronal activity patterns via VSA, then stored as fixed points in a recurrent network using Hebbian-like plasticity. Retrieval occurs via partial cues that converge to memorized attractors.
trained_components: recurrent network weights via associative memory plasticity rules

claims.statement: The model allows for efficient storage of knowledge structures such that the memorized structures as well as their individual building blocks (e.g., events and attributes) can be subsequently retrieved from partial retrieving cues.
Long-term memory of structured knowledge relies on a new principle of computation beyond the memory basins.
The model can be extended to store sequences of memories as single attractors.
Signal-to-noise analysis combined with numerical simulations demonstrate efficient storage capacity for knowledge structures represented as binary relations between events and attributes.
